summary: Simon Späti makes a case for Data Warehouse Automation (DWA) as the next logical step in an industry where automation has transformed every sector. He argues that in an era where data is the new gold and time is precious, manually building data warehouses creates unnecessary friction and errors. DWA enables teams to deliver KPIs within weeks rather than months, shifting focus from technical plumbing to actual data analysis. The core value proposition is eliminating repetitive tasks like creating staging tables, managing schema changes, and handling slowly changing dimensions—work that follows predictable patterns and begs for automation.
key_points: DWA shifts the focus from building and hunting data to actually examining and analyzing it
Repetitive DWA tasks (staging tables, schema changes, SCD handling, indexing) follow patterns that make them ideal automation candidates
Automated ETL provides consistency, proven code templates, and automatic documentation generation
Testing data warehouses manually is nearly impossible but can be automated with DWA tools
Version control enables easy rollback and change management that traditional approaches lack
money_quotes: In a society where time flys remarkably fast and data became the new gold, it's crucial to have proper analyses or even data predictive trends to navigate the company in the best direction.
It's not the time to do recurring tasks manually over again and producing additional errors.
DWA enables you to concentrate on examining the data not hunting and building them
Testing can be automated (which is almost impossible doing it in the traditional way)
We do the same thing over and over again in Data Warehousing.
It is actually hard to find a reason not to automate.
Don't waste more time and work smart at the speed of business with DWA!
key_insight: Data warehouse development is fundamentally repetitive work that follows predictable patterns, making it an ideal candidate for automation that frees teams to focus on actual data analysis rather than technical plumbing.
